# LedgerCore / LedgerBrain — x402 pay-per-use KYA, KYT, and KYE compliance API for AI agents

Run crypto address risk reports, transaction risk screening, entity due diligence, compliance risk
screening, and auditable compliance reports via x402 payments. No LedgerBrain API key required
for x402 endpoints.

Canonical API host: https://api.ledgercore.io

## Discovery

- x402 discovery: https://api.ledgercore.io/.well-known/x402
- OpenAPI: https://api.ledgercore.io/openapi.json
- Catalog: https://api.ledgercore.io/api/v1/x402/catalog
- API portal: https://api.ledgercore.io/api/v1/portals/x402
- Website: https://ledgerbrain.io/x402
- x402scan: https://www.x402scan.com/server/694cd5d6-fddf-4a2f-be96-832b649909f7
- llms.txt: https://api.ledgercore.io/llms.txt

## Paid endpoints

- POST https://api.ledgercore.io/api/v1/x402/kya/report
  KYA address risk report. Use before sending crypto to a receiving address.

- POST https://api.ledgercore.io/api/v1/x402/kyt/report
  KYT transaction risk report. Use when receiving or reviewing a crypto payment.

- POST https://api.ledgercore.io/api/v1/x402/kye/report
  KYE entity intelligence report. Use for counterparty or entity due diligence context.

## Payment

- Protocol: x402 v2
- Network: Base mainnet
- Token: USDC
- Price: 1.00 USDC per report
- No LedgerBrain API key required for x402 endpoints

## Request schemas

KYA (required: address, chain):
  { "address": "1A1zP1eP5QGefi2DMPTfTL5SLmv7Divfna", "chain": "bitcoin" }

KYT (required: txid, chain — use txid, not tx_hash):
  { "txid": "4a5e1e4baab89f3a32518a88c31bc87f618f76673e2cc77ab2127b7afdeda33b", "chain": "bitcoin" }

KYE (required: name — entity name, min 2 chars):
  { "name": "Binance" }

## x402 payment flow

1. Fetch catalog: GET https://api.ledgercore.io/api/v1/x402/catalog
2. Choose KYA for address risk, KYT for transaction risk, or KYE for entity due diligence.
3. POST to the chosen endpoint without a payment header.
4. Receive HTTP 402 Payment Required with a PAYMENT-REQUIRED header.
5. The PAYMENT-REQUIRED header contains payTo, price, network, and token.
6. Pay in USDC on Base mainnet using an x402-capable client.
7. Retry with the PAYMENT-SIGNATURE header containing the payment proof.
8. Use the returned compliance report in your own policy workflow.

## Discovery tags

KYA, KYT, KYE, crypto compliance, address risk, transaction risk, entity due diligence,
compliance reports, compliance risk screening, AML/sanctions risk context, AI agents, x402,
pay-per-use, pay-as-you-go, USDC, Base, no LedgerBrain API key required for x402 endpoints,
HTTP 402 Payment Required, blockchain intelligence, counterparty risk, payment risk screening.

## Safety note

LedgerBrain reports provide compliance intelligence and risk context. They are not legal advice,
financial advice, or a replacement for regulated compliance judgment. Compliance risk screening
includes AML/sanctions-related risk context where available in the report.
